question: [File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: '] [Constant: filterKeys.INTERNAL] Can an unprivileged external user submit filterKeys.INTERNAL with value 'true' (using isValidBooleanOpAndValue) to retrieve internal contract call results that may expose sensitive intermediate state not intended for public consumption?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: '] [Constant: filterKeys.NONCE] Can an unprivileged external user submit filterKeys.NONCE with value '0' (which passes isNonNegativeInt32 since 0 is non-negative) to retrieve all transactions with nonce 0, potentially identifying contract deployment transactions and exposing deployer addresses?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: '] [Constant: filterKeys.SCHEDULED] Can an unprivileged external user submit filterKeys.SCHEDULED with value 'false' to retrieve all unscheduled transactions, bypassing any implicit filter that hides pending scheduled transactions and exposing the full unfiltered transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: '] [Constant: filterKeys.SPENDER_ID] Can an unprivileged external user submit filterKeys.SPENDER_ID with their own account ID to enumerate all NFT allowances granted to them across all token contracts, revealing the full scope of their approved spending permissions and potentially identifying high-value targets?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: '] [Constant: filterKeys.TOKEN_TYPE] Can an unprivileged external user submit filterKeys.TOKEN_TYPE with value 'ALL' (from tokenTypeFilter.ALL) to bypass any implicit token type restriction and retrieve both fungible and non-fungible tokens in a single query, potentially causing a larger-than-expected result set?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: '] [Constant: filterKeys.TRANSACTION_TYPE] Can an unprivileged external user submit filterKeys.TRANSACTION_TYPE with a valid transaction type string that maps to a high-frequency transaction type to cause the database to return an extremely large result set, exhausting server memory?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: '] [Constant: filterKeys.TRANSACTION_HASH] Can an unprivileged external user submit filterKeys.TRANSACTION_HASH with a 32-byte all-zero hash (ETH_HASH_LENGTH = 32) to probe for the existence of a zero-hash transaction, potentially revealing information about genesis or system transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: '] [Constant: filterKeys.HASH_OR_NUMBER] Can an unprivileged external user submit filterKeys.HASH_OR_NUMBER with a value that is simultaneously valid as both a hash and a number (e.g., a short all-digit hex string) to trigger ambiguous parsing that resolves to an unintended entity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: '] [Constant: filterKeys.SLOT] Can an unprivileged external user submit filterKeys.SLOT with a 64-character hex value (maximum valid slot) combined with a 'lte' operator to retrieve all contract storage slots up to the maximum value, performing a full storage dump of a contract?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: '] [Constant: filterKeys.TOPIC_ID] Can an unprivileged external user submit filterKeys.TOPIC_ID with a value of '0.0.1' to query messages for a system topic, potentially exposing internal network messages or governance data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: '] [Constant: filterKeys.BALANCE] Can an unprivileged external user submit filterKeys.BALANCE with value 'true' (isValidBooleanOpAndValue) to force inclusion of balance data in account queries, increasing response payload size and causing memory pressure on the server?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: '] [Constant: filterKeys.CREDIT_TYPE] Can an unprivileged external user submit filterKeys.CREDIT_TYPE with value 'credit' (cryptoTransferType.CREDIT) to filter only incoming transfers, potentially revealing the full credit history of any account and enabling financial surveillance of other users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: '] [Constant: orderFilterValues.ASC / DESC] Can an unprivileged external user submit filterKeys.ORDER with a mixed-case value (e.g., 'ASC', 'Asc') that passes the isValidValueIgnoreCase check but is stored in the query differently than the canonical lowercase value, causing cache key collisions between ASC and DESC queries?
